name: Brandy Snaps
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Corn syrup or Light molasses
recipeInstructions:
1. Line a cookie sheet with heavy foil and grease the foil (use new foil for each batch). Set aside.
2. In a bowl, stir together brown sugar, melted butter, corn syrup or light molasses, and brandy.
3. Add flour, ginger, and nutmeg to the mixture. Stir until combined.
4. Drop batter from a level teaspoon, about 3 inches apart, onto the prepared cookie sheet. Bake in batches of 4 or 5 cookies at a time.
5. Bake at 350°F for 5 to 6 minutes, or until bubbly and deep golden brown.
6. Cool cookies on the sheet for about 2 minutes, or until set.
7. Remove each cookie one at a time and place upside down on a heatproof surface.
8. Immediately roll each cookie around a metal cone or the greased handle of a wooden spoon. Allow to cool completely on a wire rack.
9. If cookies harden before shaping, reheat them for about 1 minute to soften.
10. In a chilled bowl, combine filling ingredients and beat with chilled beaters on low speed until stiff peaks form.
11. Just before serving, pipe the filling into the cookies using a pastry bag fitted with a large star tip.
These make a beautiful presentation at a holiday party.
